vBRIEFInfo.author: Viz + Deft interview
vBRIEFInfo.description: Research for runtime-agnostic eve execution via DurabilityBackend

narratives.Scope: Investigate how to make eve's execution substrate runtime-agnostic by introducing a DurabilityBackend (parallel to SandboxBackend) that abstracts durable sessions, turn checkpoints, delivery inboxes, event streams, child turn jobs, and schedule triggers. Primary motivation: support self-hosted and alternative engines (Rivet agentOS actors/workflows/cron, in-memory tests) without forking the harness, compiler, or agent/ authoring model. Out of scope for this research: agentOS as sandbox only (separate track), full replacement of Nitro HTTP host, or Rivet built-in coding agents inside the same turn as eve harness.
narratives.Findings: eve already documents runtime portability at the Workflow World layer (experimental.workflow.world swaps queue/state store while keeping @workflow/core semantics). That is storage portability, not execution portability. The coupling surface is concentrated in packages/eve/src/execution/ (workflow-entry, turn-workflow, workflow-steps, workflow-runtime, hook-ownership, durable-session-store) and packages/eve/src/internal/workflow-bundle/ (use workflow/use step transforms, Vercel deploy output). The public Runtime interface (run/deliver/getEventStream) in channel/types.ts is already backend-shaped. SandboxBackend in shared/sandbox-backend.ts is the precedent: name, prewarm/create lifecycle, persisted reconnect metadata, author-pluggable factories. Rivet agentOS actors/workflows/cron overlap with eve only on durability/orchestration (features 4+9 in prior analysis); agentOS as SandboxBackend avoids that overlap. Recommended architecture: DurabilityPort (checkpoints, inboxes, event log, child jobs) implemented by DurabilityBackend adapters — vercelWorkflow() wraps current code, inMemory() for unit/integration tests, rivet() as follow-on. SessionDriver and TurnDriver hold runtime-agnostic orchestration; adapters stay thin.
narratives.DontHandRoll:
Problem: Durable checkpoint/replay semantics. Use: DurabilityBackend port with vercelWorkflow() adapter delegating to existing @workflow/core. Rationale: eve already encodes hook conflict rules, pinned parent vs latest child turn routing, NextDriverAction contract, and DurableSessionState migrations — reimplementing from scratch risks subtle HITL/delivery bugs.

Problem: Workflow state/queue storage for self-hosted eve. Use: experimental.workflow.world packages (@workflow/world-local, @workflow/world-postgres) on current eve before a full DurabilityBackend fork. Rationale: eve CHANGELOG and docs already support this path; satisfies no-Vercel-hosting without rewriting execution/.

Problem: In-process sandbox isolation. Use: SandboxBackend (agentOS, docker, etc.) — separate from DurabilityBackend. Rationale: orthogonal concerns; docs/concepts/execution-model-and-durability.md already states Nitro, Workflow world, and Sandbox backend are separate adapters.

Problem: Full durable workflow platform (Temporal/Restate). Use: only as optional future DurabilityBackend adapter, not as inline dependency in packages/eve. Rationale: AGENTS.md principle 3 — eve keeps nitro as sole runtime dep; heavy engines belong in author apps or optional peer backends.

Problem: Compile-time workflow transforms. Use: keep WorkflowBundleBuilder behind vercelWorkflow() adapter initially; do not delete until a second adapter proves the port. Rationale: scenario tests and e2e depend on Vercel workflow output shape today.
narratives.CommonPitfalls:
Pitfall: Confusing World with DurabilityBackend. What: Team swaps postgres world and expects Rivet-native actors/cron. Why: World only changes persistence for Workflow SDK. Avoid: Document DurabilityBackend as execution engine port; World remains a vercelWorkflow() implementation detail.

Pitfall: Mixing Rivet agent sessions with eve harness. What: Two LLM loops and duplicate transcripts in one turn. Why: agentOS feature 4 runs Pi/Claude inside VM. Avoid: Use agentOS core for sandbox OR durability adapter only; never createSession(pi) alongside eve tool-loop unless Pattern B is explicit product choice.

Pitfall: Breaking continuation-token exclusivity. What: Duplicate sessions or lost deliveries after backend swap. Why: hook-ownership.ts and delivery hook rekey semantics are subtle. Avoid: Port tests for claimHookOwnership, session delivery race, and competing continuation tokens before declaring adapter parity.

Pitfall: Ignoring deployment pinning. What: Parent session on old deployment, child turnWorkflow on latest — state shape drift. Why: STABLE_WORKFLOW_NAMES and deploymentId routing are eve-specific. Avoid: Encode cross-deploy behavior in DurabilityBackend capabilities matrix; rivet() adapter must document degraded or emulated modes.

Pitfall: Non-idempotent side effects across checkpoint replay. What: Double charges or duplicate emails after crash. Why: interrupted steps re-run per execution-model docs. Avoid: Acceptance criteria require idempotency guidance unchanged across backends.

Pitfall: Testing with bare vitest run. What: Stale dist/ tests give false confidence. Why: tier configs alias #* to src. Avoid: inMemory() adapter + vitest.integration.config.ts for port tests; scenario tier for bundler adapters.

Pitfall: Scope creep into research/ upstream. What: Large eve PR before spike. Why: vercel/eve is upstream framework with invariant guards. Avoid: Phase 0 spike in fork or packages/eve behind experimental flag; research/ doc for upstream proposal mirroring dynamic-model.md pattern.
narratives.IPRisk: No third-party fictional IP, game, or branded-universe terms detected in scope. Rivet agentOS and Vercel Workflow are software products — use trademarked names only as technical references, not as shipped user-facing branding. Commercial deployment of Rivet/Vercel stacks may require separate vendor agreements; that is an ops/licensing concern, not a content IP issue. No lawyer consultation triggered by heuristic.
narratives.Recommendations: 1) Short term: document DurabilityBackend in research/ for upstream eve (issue-backed) while proving port with inMemory() + vercelWorkflow() wrapper. 2) Medium: self-hosted operators use experimental.workflow.world before rivet() adapter. 3) Long: rivet() adapter only after SessionDriver/TurnDriver extract and hook/inbox port is stable. 4) Parallel: agentOS SandboxBackend stays independent (evekit doc already drafted). 5) Sizing for interview: likely Full path — multi-phase, touches execution/ + workflow-bundle + tests + docs + optional research/ PR.
